items.title: Family of Perth man raises questions over his Bali death

children.content: Cameron Hughes had been living in Bali for more than 15 years, and his family says he had been trying to extend his visa to stay close to his son before he died in an immigration detention centre.

items.title: Blind bowler's dream threatened by NDIS funding cuts

children.content: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

image.altText: A middle-aged man in a cap and sports tracksuit top stands in front of a bowling green.

items.title: Junior doctor faces court over death of man in Fremantle

children.content: A junior doctor from Perth's western suburbs appears in court charged with manslaughter following the death of a 63-year-old man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours yesterday.

image.altText: A young man in a blue hoodie and gloves smiles for the camera. There is snow and water in the background.

items.title: North Midlands annual golf day rallies support for first aid

children.content: The long wait for an ambulance in WA's Wheatbelt makes first aid critical, and these golfers are ensuring their community is well prepared.

image.altText: Four people with golf clubs stand next to a hole

items.title: Premier Roger Cook takes over defence industries in cabinet reshuffle

children.content: WA Premier Roger Cook will take on the defence industries and tourism portfolios in a cabinet reshuffle, following the departure of longstanding minister Paul Papalia.

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook stands behind a podium, with flags hanging behind him.

items.title: Jewish academic fears being met by 'axe or gun' when on campus

children.content: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

articles.title: Government to crack down on antisemitism on university campuses

children.content: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

image.altText: a wide image of sandstone arches at a university campus. people can be seen walking alongside the arches

gallery.altText: University of Wollongong sign on glass wall.

thumbnail.altText: University of Wollongong sign on glass wall.

items.title: Change to international travel as digital arrival cards roll out

children.content: A digital alternative to incoming passenger cards filled out by everyone entering Australia is to be implemented in the next two years.

image.altText: People walk under the arrivals sign at Hobart Airport

items.title: Albanese has the Coalition exactly where he wants it

children.content: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

image.altText: A close up of Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a white hard hat and a yellow high-vis vest.

items.title: How India uranium deal could unlock billions for resources powerhouse of WA

children.content: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.

items.title: Australians paying 'unjustified' fees to pharmacies, report says

children.content: Taxpayers are missing out on cheaper medicines due to backroom deals by one of Australia's most powerful lobby groups, according to a new report.

image.altText: A script counter at a pharmacy, with an unidentifiable man waiting in line.

items.title: Markets on edge as US-Iran war flares again, oil price under pressure

children.content: Wall Street rallied on Friday before Gulf hostilities flared again. ASX futures point to a gain this morning. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Book inspires Wajarri man to ditch mining for leadership

children.content: Board meetings and navigating native title were not on Kurt McIntosh's radar until he stumbled upon a book at the airport.

image.altText: Kurt is sitting on a tree root within a dry river bed holding Obama's book.  Kurt is wearing a grey jumper and purple shorts

items.title: Businesses caught between Labor's trust tax and stamp duty

children.content: Small businesses affected by the change are in limbo as states decide whether to waive stamp duty fees, with industry groups warning relief will be limited unless they sign on.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

items.title: Sinner triumphs over Zverev for second consecutive Wimbledon win

children.content: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

articles.title: Hewitt drops Wimbledon boys' title after epic battle against US star

children.content: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

image.altText: Cruz Hewitt looking frustrated while holding his racquet

articles.title: Nosková downs 'ex-friend' Muchová to win all-Czech Wimbledon final

children.content: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

image.altText: A young woman beams as she holds a giant sterling silver plate in front of a crowd.

items.title: How setting small, specific goals can help reduce your drinking

children.content: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

items.title: WA shire tells hospitality business to remove 'unsafe' roadside signs

children.content: Businesses in a popular West Australian tourist destination say a move by the local shire to ban some roadside signs will cut traffic to their stores.

items.title: Tour de Cure reflects on Scolyer's enduring legacy ahead of memorial service

children.content: As Mark Beretta rode his bike up a steep Tasmanian mountain in March with all his might, it was none other than Richard Scolyer who overtook him.

items.title: Roger Cook flexes his power with captain's call on new minister
